page: Microsoft Azure E48ds_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the Edsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with x86_64 architecture. The processor operates at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz and includes a 480 MB L3 cache. This instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM, establishing an 8.0 GB per core memory-to-vCPU ratio. Local storage is bundled with 2835 GB of capacity, and the instance is capable of utilizing Premium Storage. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-threaded and memory-bound performance. The instance achieves a PassMark CPU score of 64,146.76 and a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 272.18 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25% tier. Single-threaded gzip compression is highly efficient, reaching 35.53 GB/sec, which falls into the top 10% tier. Memory bandwidth tests show read-only small block cached speeds of 3,532.63 GB/sec, while large block uncached read and write speeds reach 162.68 GB/sec and 66.17 GB/sec, respectively. For database and key-value operations, the server demonstrates strong performance with 76.28 million Redis SET operations per second and a PassMark database operations score of 20,508.51.

With its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and substantial local storage, the E48ds_v7 offers high resource density suitable for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. It is well-suited for large relational databases, in-memory caching layers, and data analytics. While it lacks GPU acceleration, making it weak for large-scale LLM text generation, it delivers strong prompt processing speeds for smaller models, such as 214.51 tokens/sec on a 7B model. The combination of dedicated Intel Xeon compute resources, a massive L3 cache, and fast local storage provides a balanced, cost-efficient profile for enterprise database hosting and high-throughput web serving without the overhead of specialized accelerator hardware.
description: Microsoft Azure E48ds_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Edsv7 family, powered by a 48-vCPU Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz. It features 384.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 1:8 vCPU-to-memory ratio, alongside 2835 GB of local storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance delivers strong performance across multi-threaded operations, highlighted by a PassMark CPU score of 64,146.76 and a top-tier single-threaded gzip compression speed of 35.53 GB/sec. It also demonstrates high throughput in database environments, processing 76.28 million Redis SET operations per second. Lacking GPU acceleration, it is not designed for heavy AI text generation but excels in memory-intensive workloads. The E48ds_v7 is highly suited for relational databases, in-memory analytics, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E48ds_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 48 vCPUs, 384 GB of RAM, and 2835 GB of local storage, delivering strong database and multi-threaded performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E48ds_v7 is a memory-optimized VM with 48 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 2835 GB local storage, built for databases and enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-density, memory-optimized virtual machine with fast local storage and strong multi-threaded processing for demanding enterprise database workloads.
bullet_points: 48 vCPUs and 384.0 GB RAM providing an optimal 1:8 memory-to-vCPU ratio.
2835 GB of fast local storage with Premium Storage capability.
Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor operating at 4.2 GHz with 480 MB L3 cache.
Top-tier single-threaded gzip compression performance of 35.53 GB/sec.
Strong database performance with 76.28 million Redis SET operations per second.
Best suited for relational databases, in-memory caching, and data analytics.
